title: Tempting Thick Greek Tofu Avgolemono
description: A malty, creamy, and adventurous Greek dish featuring gelatine leafs and bonito-shave, made vegan-friendly in a tagine-pot, avgolemono style.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Combine the orzo and vegetable broth in the tagine-pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes or until the orzo is tender.
Whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, malt syrup, and bonito-shave in a small bowl.
Add the cubed tofu to the tagine-pot and stir to combine with the orzo mixture.
Pour the lemon juice mixture over the tofu and orzo mixture and stir to combine.
Add the gelatine leafs to the tagine-pot and stir to dissolve.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the tagine-pot to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes or until the mixture is hot and bubbly.
Remove the tagine-pot from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
